task_name: Workspace bootstrap in auth and shared membership helpers

strengths: Pure function createSignupWorkspacePlan is cleanly separated and unit-testable with no DB/Express dependency (auth.ts:26-41), matching the codebase's pure-helper convention.
Personal workspace, owner membership, and user creation share a single BEGIN/COMMIT transaction with ROLLBACK on failure (auth.ts:116-173), satisfying the transactional-signup must-have.
Pending invites are fetched and surfaced via the plan but never inserted as memberships, correctly leaving invites unconsumed on signup (auth.ts:128-140).
Cap helper returns the exact spec error copy 'You've reached the workspace limit (10).' via a single CAP_ERROR_MESSAGE constant (routes.ts:16-28), avoiding magic strings.
T2 diff is correctly scoped: routes.ts adds only WORKSPACE_LIMIT/CAP_ERROR_MESSAGE/getWorkspaceCapacity/serializeWorkspaceList; route handlers and auth checks belong to T3.
All 4 tests (auth.test.ts, workspaces.test.ts) pass; tests follow the plan's TDD ordering.
Async error path (throw err, auth.ts:170) is safe and consistent with the existing untry-catched login handler because the project runs Express 5, which forwards async rejections to error middleware.
